title: Thiruvali-Thirunagari
tagline: Thiruvali-Thirunagari — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Thiruvali - Thirunagari Temples are paired Hindu temples dedicated to Vishnu located 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) from Sirkali in Tamil Nadu, India, and 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) from ea
overview.description: The Thiruvali - Thirunagari Temples are paired Hindu temples dedicated to Vishnu located 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) from Sirkali in Tamil Nadu, India, and 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) from each other. It is one of the Divya Desams, the 108 temples of Vishnu revered by the 12 poet saints, or Alvars. Unlike other Divya Desams where a single shrine is referred, this pair of temples is referred to together in all of the 41 pasurams (hymns). These temples follow the Tenkalai mode of worship.

topPlaces.name: Vedarajan temple, Thirunagari
topPlaces.description: Vedarajan Temple in Thirunagari, a village in Mayiladuthurai district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Naalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Vedarajan and his consort Lakshmi as Amruthavalli.

topPlaces.name: Velladainatha Swami Temple
topPlaces.description: Velladainatha Swami Temple is a Hindu temple situated in the village of Thirukkarukavur in the Mayiladuthurai district of Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Azhagiyasingar Temple, Thiruvali
topPlaces.description: The Alagiyasingar Temple in Thiruvali, a village in Mayiladuthurai district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Alagiyasingar and his consort Lakshmi as Purnavalli.

topPlaces.name: Thiruthevanartthogai
topPlaces.description: Thiruthevanartthogai or Madhava Perumal Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu located in Tirunangur, a village in the outskirts of Sirkaḻi in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Madhava Perumal and his consort Lakshmi as Kadalmagal.

topPlaces.name: Rudrakodisvarar Temple, Sempankudi
topPlaces.description: Rudrakodisvarar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located at Sempankudi in Mayiladuthurai district in Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Thirumanikkoodam
topPlaces.description: Thirumanikkoodam or Varadaraja Perumal Temple is located in Thirunangur, a village in the outskirts of Sirkaḻi in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu.

topPlaces.name: Thiruvellakkulam
topPlaces.description: Thiruvellakkulam (also called Annan Perumal Temple or Annankoil) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu located in Thirunangur, a village in the outskirts of Sirkazhi in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Annan Perumal and his consort Lakshmi as Alamermangai.

topPlaces.name: Thiruchsemponsey
topPlaces.description: Thiruchsemponsey or Perarulaalan Perumal Temple is dedicated to Hindu god Vishnu located in Thirunangur, a village in the outskirts of Sirkazhi in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries AD. It is one of the 108 Divya Desam dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Peralulalan and his consort Lakshmi as Allimalar Nachiyar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thiruvali-Thirunagari?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thiruvali-Thirunagari is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (129 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (130 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thiruvali-Thirunagari?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thiruvali-Thirunagari at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thiruvali-Thirunagari?
faq.a: Stays in Thiruvali-Thirunagari start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thiruvali-Thirunagari?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vedarajan temple, Thirunagari, Velladainatha Swami Temple, Azhagiyasingar Temple, Thiruvali, Thiruthevanartthogai, Rudrakodisvarar Temple, Sempankudi.
